response:
spring brings hope to the city where taxis move fast and spring brings joy to travelers who pay fair fares. The fare equals ten yuan if distance is four or less. Extra cost adds two yuan per kilometer beyond the base limit. This rule applies to all trips taken by drivers in the town. The total price rises as the distance grows larger each day.

The driver stopped for at most twenty minutes during the fifteen kilometer trip. Stopping time converts to distance at five minutes per single kilometer. A thirty eight yuan fare implies six extra kilometers beyond the base four. Six extra kilometers means thirty minutes of equivalent distance added. The actual travel was fifteen so the stops must be twenty minutes max. spring fills the air with fresh scents while we calculate these numbers. spring returns again to remind us of the math behind the fare.
